reasoning: The flagged script (ambiancetoradiance.sh) shows poor shell scripting practices but no actual malicious behavior. Qwen's 'obfuscation' claim misinterprets HTML entity encoding in code comments, and command injection risk is theoretical in controlled build environments where filenames are package-controlled.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged normal theme scripts as unsafe due to misinterpreting code formatting as obfuscation and overestimating filename injection risks in controlled build contexts
false_positives: ambiancetoradiance.sh: 'Obfuscation' claims stem from HTML entities in comments; command injection requires malicious filenames not present in package context
learnings: Improve shell script analysis to distinguish between actual obfuscation vs. formatting artifacts
Add context-aware risk assessment for filename handling in build scripts
Prioritize review of executable scripts referenced in PKGBUILD over theme assets
